title: veronica corriganii
side_effects: Veronica corriganii can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness due to its high concentration of volatile oils. Prolonged consumption of the plant's extracts can also lead to kidney damage, liver failure, and stomach ulcers in severe cases. In addition, some individuals may experience allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and swelling, after coming into contact with the plant's sap or essential oils. If not handled and used correctly, Veronica corriganii can cause these and other adverse health effects.
